  "findings": [
    "PVC flue slope is back-pitched, which will trap acidic condensate in the venting system instead of draining it back to the furnace condensate management system."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work on the venting system immediately.",
    "Re-slope the PVC flue piping to ensure a minimum pitch of 1/4 inch per foot slope back to the appliance or condensate drain.",
    "Verify proper support spacing to prevent future sagging or back-pitching."
  ],
  "rationale": "A back-pitched condensing flue pipe traps condensate, which can restrict flue gas flow, cause the furnace pressure switch to trip, or lead to acidic condensate leaking and damaging the building or equipment. This violates standard installation codes requiring a continuous slope back to the appliance.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801",
    "NFPA 54"
  ]
}
